_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX state-legislature batch: angelia-orr (R HD-14), borris-miles (D SD-13), paul-dyson (R HD-14), sarah-eckhardt (D SD-14 -> 2026 Comptroller nominee), molly-cook (D SD-15). State tier; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence.

profile.background: Republican Texas State Rep, HD-14 (Bosque/Freestone/Falls/Leon/Hill/Limestone + part of McLennan); in office since Jan 2023. Former Itasca school board member, Hill County District Clerk, rancher/small-business owner. Texas A&M 1992. Won 2026 GOP primary; advances to Nov 3 2026 general.

angelia-orr@TX.notes_append: Refined 2026-06 (evidence_state). Pro-life: Texas Alliance for Life 2025 scorecard 96%, endorsed by TAFL+LIFE PAC; Texas Right to Life 86 (2025)/63 (2023). House sponsor of SB8 (2025) 'Texas Women's Privacy Act' bathroom bill (restricts gov/school facilities to sex at birth) - HARD on biological-sex item. Voted YES on SB2 (2025) ESA/school-choice voucher (after a documented 2023 anti-voucher reversal). Endorsed by NRA-PVF, TSRA-PAC, Texas Gun Rights PAC (2022); 2023 pro-gun votes (HB1760, HB2291, HB3137). No direct evidence found on marriage definition, euthanasia, CBDC/ESG, federalism, border, or election-machine items - left null.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 2025 scorecard 96%; endorsed by TAFL + LIFE PAC; TXRTL 86 (2025) - documented pro-life voting record.

2.note: House sponsor of SB8 (2025) 'Texas Women's Privacy Act' restricting government/school facilities to biological sex at birth; signed into law.

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (2025), the ESA/school-choice voucher program.
Endorsed by NRA-PVF, TSRA-PAC, and Texas Gun Rights PAC (2022, constitutional-carry org); voted for pro-gun bills HB1760/HB2291/HB3137 in 2023.

1.note: Gun-rights group endorsements + 2023 votes against firearm restrictions (narrowed gun-free zones, blocked local firearm-insurance mandates).

profile.background: Democratic Texas State Senator, SD-13 (Houston/Sunnyside); in Senate since 2017, prior TX House 2007-2017. Insurance/real-estate developer. Running for re-election in 2026 (advanced from March primary; Nov 3 2026 general).

borris-miles@TX.notes_append: Refined 2026-06 (evidence_state). Consistently progressive voting record. Planned Parenthood 'Champion of Women's Health' award; Texas Alliance for Life 2025 scorecard 57% (voted against pro-life priorities). Equality Texas A+ rating. Voted AGAINST school-choice/ESA vouchers (2023). Strongly opposed SB1 (2021) election bill, calling it voter suppression. On guns the deviation is PERSONAL not legislative: a CHL holder who shot a would-be thief in self-defense (2007), but he voted AGAINST castle-doctrine expansion (2007) and AGAINST HB1927 constitutional carry (2021, party-line 18-13). No documented conservative VOTE found. Null on stem-cell, euthanasia, CBDC/ESG, federalism, border.

0.note: Planned Parenthood 'Champion of Women's Health' award; Texas Alliance for Life 2025 scorecard 57%, voting against pro-life priorities (Life of the Mother Act, abortion-travel ban) - documented pro-abortion-rights stance.

4.note: Recognized with Planned Parenthood's 'Champion of Women's Health' award - documented alignment with the abortion industry.

0.note: Equality Texas A+ rating - documented pro-LGBTQ record contrary to one-man/one-woman marriage position.

4.note: Equality Texas A+ rating - supports LGBTQ policy promotion.

1.note: Strongly opposed SB1 (2021), which strengthened voter-ID/mail-ballot ID requirements; called it voter suppression.

3.note: Opposed SB1 (2021) restrictions on mail-in voting, drop boxes, and drive-thru/24-hour voting.

0.note: Voted AGAINST HB1927 (2021) constitutional/permitless carry; Senate passage was party-line 18-13 with all Democrats opposed.

3.note: Voted against the castle-doctrine / stand-your-ground deadly-force expansion bill (2007).

profile.religion: Catholic background (attended St. Joseph's Catholic School; current affiliation not confirmed)
profile.background: Republican Texas State Rep, HD-14 (Brazos County - Bryan/College Station, Texas A&M). First elected 2024, assumed office Jan 2025; succeeded John Raney. Attorney and general counsel for family energy firm; Texas A&M B.S./MBA, J.D. South Texas College of Law. Endorsed by Gov. Abbott. 2026 incumbent.

paul-dyson@TX.notes_append: Refined 2026-06 (evidence_state). First-term member (since Jan 2025); thinner record. Texas Right to Life 100% (2025) and Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025). Voted YES on SB2 (2025) ESA voucher and to table the anti-voucher amendment. Authored HB1871 (2025) increasing penalties for attempted capital murder of peace officers - HARD pro-law-enforcement. Campaign site explicitly states 'staunch advocate for Constitutional Carry.' SB14 (minor gender-transition ban) passed in 2023 before he took office - no vote. No documented marriage, election, CBDC/ESG, federalism, or specific border votes - left null.

0.note: Texas Right to Life 2025 = 100% and Texas Alliance for Life 2025 = 100% (scored on SB31/SB33/SB1388/HB7 abortion-pill enforcement etc.).
Voted YES on SB2 (2025) ESA/voucher and voted to table the anti-voucher (Talarico) amendment, Apr 17 2025.
Authored HB1871 (2025) increasing penalties for attempted capital murder of peace officers - affirmative pro-law-enforcement legislation.
Campaign site: 'staunch advocate for Constitutional Carry... the broadest possible freedom' on the right to bear arms - clear self-stated position.

set.office: Texas State Senator (sitting · 2026 Democratic nominee for Texas Comptroller)

profile.background: Democratic Texas State Senator, SD-14 (Austin/Travis County); in office since 2020, term to 2029. Former Travis County Commissioner and County Judge; daughter of late U.S. Rep. Bob Eckhardt; JD/MPA, UT-Austin. NOT seeking SD-14 re-election - won the March 2026 Democratic primary for Texas Comptroller (~64%); faces Republican Don Huffines in the Nov 3 2026 general.
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06 via Ballotpedia + Texas Tribune (Dec 2025): currently holds SD-14, is the 2026 Democratic Comptroller nominee.

sarah-eckhardt@TX.notes_append: Refined 2026-06 (evidence_state). CANDIDACY CURRENCY: not running for SD-14 re-election; sitting senator running for statewide Comptroller (2026 Dem nominee vs Huffines). Documented progressive record: endorsed by Annie's List and Planned Parenthood Texas Votes, condemned SB8 abortion law; opposed SB14 (2023 minor gender-transition ban) on the floor; voted NO on SB2 (2025) ESA vouchers; opposed SB1515 (2023 Ten Commandments in classrooms), filing amendments to add Islam/Buddhism content; opposed SB1 (2021) election bill and joined the DC quorum-break; opposed HB1927 (2021) constitutional carry. No conservative-aligned position found. Null on stem-cell, euthanasia, CBDC/ESG, federalism, border.

0.note: Endorsed by Annie's List and Planned Parenthood Texas Votes; condemned SB8 and advocates 'restoration of safe and legal reproductive healthcare' - documented pro-abortion-rights stance.

4.note: Endorsed by Annie's List and Planned Parenthood Texas Votes - abortion-industry-aligned PAC support.

2.note: Opposed SB14 (2023 ban on gender-transition care for minors); floor statement defending 'gender-affirming care to transgender kids' - affirms transgender ideology.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025) ESA voucher; 'Public dollars should fund public schools.'

3.note: Opposed SB1515 (2023 Ten Commandments in public classrooms): 'Texans do not want religion crammed down their throat by their government.'

4.note: Filed amendments to add Islam's Five Pillars and Buddhism's Eightfold Path to the Ten Commandments bill - contrary to opposing state promotion of non-Christian displays/curricula.

1.note: Opposed SB1 (2021) voter-ID/mail-ballot ID provisions; joined the DC quorum-break to block it.

3.note: Opposed SB1 (2021) restrictions on mail-in voting and drive-thru/24-hour voting.

0.note: Opposed HB1927 (2021) constitutional/permitless carry, citing loss of LTC background-check filters.

profile.background: Democratic Texas State Senator, SD-15 (Houston). Won May 2024 special election and Nov 2024 general for a full term to Jan 2029 (NOT on the 2026 ballot). ER nurse, MPH/MSN Johns Hopkins; activist (Stop TxDOT I-45). First openly LGBTQ+ (bisexual) Texas senator.

molly-cook@TX.notes_append: Refined 2026-06 (evidence_state). Consistent progressive across every category (own campaign + record). Self-disclosed having an abortion; campaign supports abortion access and insurance coverage; endorsed by Annie's List. First openly bisexual TX senator (Victory Fund); supports trans 'dignity.' Voted AGAINST SB2 (2025) ESA voucher; opposes charter expansion. Campaign issues page: supports online registration / 24-hour / drive-thru voting and 'removing restrictive voting laws'; frames 'crime must be understood as a public health issue'; supports universal background checks, banning person-to-person gun sales, raising assault-rifle age to 21. No conservative-aligned position found. Null on stem-cell, euthanasia, CBDC/ESG, federalism, border.

0.note: Self-disclosed having an abortion ('a personal choice'); campaign supports abortion access and insurance coverage - documented pro-abortion-rights stance.
First openly bisexual TX senator, LGBTQ Victory Fund-backed and pro-marriage-equality - contrary to one-man/one-woman marriage position.

2.note: Campaign: trans Texans deserve 'dignity,' opposes 'discriminatory policymaking' - affirms transgender ideology.

4.note: Pro-LGBTQ platform - supports LGBTQ policy promotion.

0.note: Voted AGAINST SB2 (2025) ESA voucher, calling it 'a fundamental betrayal of our constitutional obligation' to public schools; also opposes charter expansion.

2.note: Supports online registration, 24-hour and drive-thru voting - contrary to single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Supports expanded voting methods (drive-thru/24-hour) and 'removing restrictive voting laws.'
Campaign: 'Crime must be understood as a public health issue' - the exact frame the rubric identifies as treating violent crime as a public-health excuse.

0.note: Supports universal background checks and gun-control measures - contrary to permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Supports banning person-to-person gun sales, universal background checks, and raising the assault-rifle purchase age to 21.
